title: Clarke steps down as Scotland boss after World Cup exit
excerpt: Steve Clarke resigns as Scotland head coach following the nation's group exit from the World Cup.
summary: Steve Clarke has resigned as head coach following Scotland's exit from the World Cup. The Scottish FA announced his departure in a statement just moments after Croatia's win over Ghana on Saturday, which sealed Scotland's fate. BBC Scotland has learned the players were told on Saturday with Clarke and the squad still at their Charlotte base.

title: Knockouts are here - what is England's path to the final?
excerpt: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after qualifying for the knockout stages.
summary: England drew with Ghana but beat Croatia and Panama to top Group L with seven points England have qualified for the knockout stages of the World Cup by winning their group. The Three Lions weren't always in top form, but with seven points from three matches, did enough to finish ahead of Croatia in second, third-placed Ghana, and bottom side Panama.

title: What you need to know about England's opponents DR Congo
excerpt: England will face DR Congo in the round of 32. Here is what you need to know about them.
summary: Yoane Wissa has now scored three goals for DR Congo in this World Cup Get the lowdown on DR Congo, England's World Cup opponents in the round of 32. Fifty-two years after a chastening World Cup debut, DR Congo have earned redemption by qualifying for the knockout stages for the first time.

title: Best team, moments, matches and players of World Cup group stage
excerpt: As the World Cup group stages come to an end, BBC Sport reporters pick their highlights so far.
summary: Lionel Messi is playing in his sixth World Cup for Argentina After 72 matches across three countries, 48 teams who qualified for the 2026 World Cup have been whittled down to 32. Records have been broken by some of the star players who have delivered on football's biggest stage, while new heroes have emerged.

title: Bellingham and Kane strike as England seal top spot with World Cup win over Panama
excerpt: England are not going to win the World Cup playing like this. They will not get very far in the knockout rounds unless they can sharpen up. But at least they have made it into them, the dream still alive, after they secured top spot in Group L on a slow-burn occasion when the result meant everyth
summary: England are not going to win the World Cup playing like this. They will not get very far in the knockout rounds unless they can sharpen up. But at least they have made it into them, the dream still alive, after they secured top spot in Group L on a slow-burn occasion when the result meant everything. Thomas Tuchel needed to restore momentum after the bore draw against Ghana in game two, to rekindle the excitement from the opening game against Croatia, which England won 4-2. The manager wanted to prove a point. It did not really happen and for the opening 45 minutes it was even possible to fear the worst. Were England going to fail to prise apart another obdurate opponent? Tuchel’s team were better after the interval. They knew that they might need only one goal and relief was the overriding emotion when Jude Bellingham stepped up. It was an expertly steered low volley from Bukayo Saka’s corner for the breakthrough, although Tuchel’s reaction was instructive. He did not celebrate. He looked annoyed.

title: Late drama sends Austria and Algeria into World Cup knockouts to break Iran hearts
excerpt: Before a ball was kicked, this match already had nicknames. Some deemed it a telltale biscotto – an Italian classic implying something that has to be baked twice; one cooperating with the other. Or maybe you preferred the “Disgrace of Kansas City”, a new spin on the “Disgrace of Gijón”,
summary: Before a ball was kicked, this match already had nicknames. Some deemed it a telltale biscotto – an Italian classic implying something that has to be baked twice; one cooperating with the other. Or maybe you preferred the “Disgrace of Kansas City”, a new spin on the “Disgrace of Gijón”, the 1982 World Cup match in which West Germany settled for a 1-0 win over Austria in a result that qualified both teams for the knockout round while eliminating Algeria. But what unfolded here was something else altogether. A new classic of the genre. Call it the “Missouri Compromise” if you must, but even that somehow underplays the drama and wild swings of momentum that exhausted the players and coaches who took part, delighting the fans who were lucky enough to witness it. On a steamy night in the American midwest, Algeria and Austria battled gamely, showing heart and desire for most if not all 90-plus minutes en route to a back-and-forth 3-3 draw that qualified both teams for the knockout round. Their continued World Cup journeys came at the expense of Iran, who just needed a winner – any winner – to emerge in this wild match to keep their tournament alive. To have been a fly on the wall of their Tijuana hotel, watching as their World Cup fate teetered and tottered, their jubilation at Riyad Mahrez’s goal in the third minute of added time abruptly ended by a Sasa Kalajdzic header with practically the last action of the match.

title: Lionel Messi steps off bench and scores to cap Argentina’s World Cup win over Jordan
excerpt: Give Lionel Messi half an hour and he will sprinkle some magic to remember for ever. Most of the crowd under this vast roof had been afflicted with their own strain of Messidependencia for two-thirds of this game, whose low stakes had been reflected in both tempo and ambience. Eve
summary: Give Lionel Messi half an hour and he will sprinkle some magic to remember for ever. Most of the crowd under this vast roof had been afflicted with their own strain of Messidependencia for two-thirds of this game, whose low stakes had been reflected in both tempo and ambience. Everything before their idol’s arrival felt almost self-consciously like a prelude, played out in the certainty that a crescendo would follow. There was no need to worry about anything else here: why not get ready to bask in seeing a little more history being made? You can set your watch by Messi, even if that does not usually mean winding it to the 60th minute. He had agreed with Lionel Scaloni that, with Argentina already guaranteed the spoils in Group J, that he would not be unleashed until then. After all, no chances should be taken for the deadest of rubbers. From the moment he came on this felt like an occasion, a moment in lore, another precious chance to distil the admiration for a legend of advancing years. So Messi delivered and sent another flock of starry-eyed devotees beaming into the muggy Texas night. Messi had already wafted a free-kick too high, the distance unaccommodating, when he was pulled down 22 yards from goal. The position, almost dead centre, could not have been more inviting and its exploitation felt inevitable. Even so the outcome was slightly curious, Messi whipping the ball around Jordan’s wall with enough bend that it nestled in the middle of Yazeed Abulaila’s net. The kee...

title: Colombia take top spot over Portugal after draw in battle of World Cup heavyweights
excerpt: While wonderful and typical theatre, it was plainly ridiculous. “I’m back!” roared Cristiano Ronaldo, as if playing a key part in Portugal’s dismissal of Uzbekistan was in any way sig
summary: While wonderful and typical theatre, it was plainly ridiculous. “I’m back!” roared Cristiano Ronaldo, as if playing a key part in Portugal’s dismissal of Uzbekistan was in any way significant. Ronaldo toiled against DR Congo and was comfortably kept quiet here by Colombia. The stars have turned up at this World Cup. Ronaldo, though, still has a way to go if seeking to prove his omnipresence at this tournament will not hinder Portuguese hopes of glory. Nobody other than Father Time has chased down Ronaldo. An alternative quote, from Trainspotting, feels more apposite than what the 41-year-old bawled into a camera as Uzbekistan were swatted aside. “Well, at one time, you’ve got it, then you lose it, and it’s gone for ever,” observed Sick Boy in an Edinburgh park. Add Ronaldo to a list that, as per the film scene, includes David Bowie, Lou Reed, Charlie Nicholas and Malcolm McLaren. Eclectic company. Ronaldo actually wants to be in the same discussion as Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland. He once was. Now? It is a tricky point to enforce. Colombia were the better team in this entertaining fixture. It was curious, in fact, that it ended scoreless. Colombia believed they had won the match in stoppage time, with a Davinson Sánchez header that was ruled out for offside. If at a more material World Cup moment, it would be a decision to create rather a lot of fuss. Colombia must improve on ruthlessness if seeking to make a proper World Cup impact. Still, topping this sect...

title: Austria survive huge scare to progress with Algeria and knock Iran out
excerpt: Super-sub Sasa Kalajdzic saved Austria's World Cup with a last-gasp equaliser after Algeria had stunned their Group J rivals deep into stoppage time in a thrilling 3-3 draw, which saw both sides progress to the last 32 and Iran knocked out.
summary: GEHA Field at Arrowhead StadiumAttendance: Attendance69,045. Match report as Austria come up with last-gasp equaliser after being floored by Riyad Mahrez's injury-time strike which would have knocked them out of World Cup; Draw sees both progress to last 32 where Group J runners-up Austria face Spain and third-place Algeria meet Switzerland Super-sub Sasa Kalajdzic saved Austria's World Cup with a last-gasp equaliser after Algeria had stunned their Group J rivals deep into stoppage time in a thrilling 3-3 draw, which saw both sides progress to the last 32 and Iran knocked out.

But will it all go according to plan, or do you think there will be some surprises as the pressure ramps up in the closing stages of the tournament?

title: Mingueza vive sus últimas horas como céltico
excerpt: Cerrará una etapa de cuatro años notables
summary: Dentro de apenas un par de días, si nadie lo remedia como así parece, Óscar Mingueza dejará de ser oficialmente jugador del Celta. Atrás quedarán cuatro muy buenos años del futbolista en Vigo.El defensa catalán llegó a la ciudad gallega un 2 de agosto de 2022 y desde prácticamente el principio se hizo con un puesto en el once, siendo un fijo en el mismo. Esa condición la perdió los últimos meses, en una situación en la que su concentración estaba puesta más en el futuro.Cuatro años en los que su rendimiento ha sido notable, disputando un total de 147 partidos oficiales, marcando 7 goles y dando 15 asistencias. Tiempo en el cual también fue convocado por por la selección española con la que suma 4 internacionalidades.Ha sido Óscar Mingueza un futbolista que también ha sumado en el vestuario, que se ha integrado en la ciudad y ha mantenido una excelente relación con Claudio Giráldez.Su posición, de todas formas, está muy bien cubierta en la plantilla con futbolistas como Sergio Carreira, Javi Rueda o Álvaro Núñez, que ya llegó en enero en previsión de la marcha de Óscar Mingueza.

title: La operación salida del Málaga, a toda vela
excerpt: Víctor García también y Javi Montero también salen del club boquerón
summary: Víctor García también y Javi Montero también salen del club boquerón Se cumple justo una semana desde que el Málaga consiguiese el ascenso en Almería. Desde entonces, los blanquiazules no han perdido ni un minuto en la planificación de la próxima temporada. El Málaga quiere reestructurar parte del bloque que consiguió el ascenso a Primera y Segunda. Es por eso que los malaguistas han optado por no renovar la mayoría de contratos que finalizaban, especialmente los de aquellos jugadores que no han sido titulares en el ascenso del equipo de Funes a la máxima categoría del fútbol español.En pocos días, el Málaga ha anunciado tres salidas. A la de Jokin Gabilondo, que fue el primero, se han unido las de Víctor García y Javi Montero. Ambos defensas han sido ya despedido con honores por el club de Martiricos en comunicado oficial. Víctor García finalizaba contrato el próximo 30 de junio y no será renovado por la entidad blanquiazul. Tal y como avanzó Radio MARCA Málaga, el lateral izquierdo está muy cerca de convertirse en nuevo jugador del Real Zaragoza, donde afrontaría un nuevo reto en un club histórico. Víctor García pone así fin a su etapa en La Rosaleda, dejando un recuerdo imborrable entre la afición malaguista. Su nombre quedará ligado para siempre al ascenso a Segunda División, ya que fue quien puso el centro al área en la acción que acabó con el gol del 2-2 en Tarragona.En el caso de Javi Montero, el jugador contaba con una cláusula de renovación en caso de ascenso si lleg...

title: Akor Adams sobre los rumores de un posible traspaso: "No me tomo esas cosas en serio"
excerpt: El delantero del Sevilla fue vinculado recientemente al Venezia
summary: El delantero del Sevilla fue vinculado recientemente al Venezia Akor Adams solo piensa en el Sevilla. El nigeriano es uno de los futbolistas señalados como posible venta en esta mercado y recientemente fue vinculado al Venezia. En Italia incluso hablaban de un acuerdo por el jugador, por una oferta superior a los diez millones de euros. En Nervión piden más de 20. Su valor de mercado, según Transfermarkt, es de 15 millones. El '9' nervionense ha salido al paso de los rumores asegurando que no está pendiente de los mismos. "Es normal que durante este periodo haya rumores de movimientos de transferencias para un montón de jugadores y no sólo míos. Estoy contento de que mi trabajo sea reconocido pero no hay nada. No he hablado con mi agente y no me tomo muy en serio estas cosas porque ahora mismo es tiempo para relajarme y recargar las pilas para la próxima temporada", apuntaba en unas declaraciones efectuadas a Footy-Africa.

summary: Senesi recibió una durísima patada en el rostro dentro del área y el árbitro no dudó en señalar la pena máxima tras una acción tan peligrosa como clara.

title: Mondiali, il recap del Gruppo G: solo 5 punti per la capolista. Iran beffato dalla Nuova Zelanda
excerpt: Il Gruppo G dei Mondiali 2026 è stato il più equilibrato dell'intera fase a gironi.
summary: Il Gruppo G dei Mondiali 2026 è stato il più equilibrato dell'intera fase a gironi. A conquistare il primo posto è stato il Belgio con appena cinque punti, il bottino più basso tra tutte le vincitrici dei dodici raggruppamenti. I Diavoli Rossi hanno preceduto l'Egitto esclusivamente grazie a una migliore differenza reti, dopo un percorso identico: vittoria contro la Nuova Zelanda e pareggi sia nello scontro diretto che contro l'Iran.Anche l'Egitto ha quindi chiuso imbattuto, assicurandosi il secondo posto e la qualificazione ai sedicesimi di finale senza particolari affanni, pur vedendo sfumare il primato per il saldo reti. La grande beffa è invece per l'Iran. Tre pareggi in altrettante partite hanno lasciato gli asiatici a quota tre punti, un bottino insufficiente per rientrare tra le migliori terze. Decisivo il pareggio contro la Nuova Zelanda, che ha impedito all'Iran di fare il salto di qualità: la nazionale iraniana ha terminato al nono posto nella graduatoria delle migliori terze, risultando la prima esclusa dalla fase a eliminazione diretta. Chiude infine all'ultimo posto la Nuova Zelanda, che evita almeno lo zero in classifica grazie al pareggio conquistato contro l'Iran, senza però riuscire a rientrare nella corsa qualificazione. CLASSIFICA GRUPPO G Belgio: 5 punti (3 partite giocata; 6 gol fatti, 2 gol subiti) Egitto: 5 punti (3 partite giocate; 5 gol fatti, 3 gol subiti) Iran: 3 punti (3 partite giocate, 3 gol fatti, 3 gol subiti) Nuova Zelanda: 1 punto (3 partite ...

title: Catania, scelto il mister per la prossima stagione: sarà Emilio Longo. I dettagli
excerpt: Il Catania ha scelto il suo allenatore in vista della prossima stagione.
summary: Il Catania ha scelto il suo allenatore in vista della prossima stagione. Obiettivo risalita immediata dopo l'uscita nelle semifinali dei playoff per mano dell'Ascoli nel passato campionato. Per tentare la risalita è stato scelto Emilio Longo, ex Crotone. Secondo quanto appreso dalla nostra redazione, il mister firmerà un contratto biennale con il club rossoazzurro.

title: Il centrocampo del Milan rischia di cambiare quasi completamente
excerpt: Rivoluzione in vista per il Milan.
summary: Rivoluzione in vista per il Milan. Non solo in dirigenza, non soltanto in panchina, ma ovviamente anche tra i giocatori. E il rischio più grande è che tra i vari reparti, il centrocampo sia quello più a rischio scossoni: la linea mediana rischia di cambiare quasi completamente. Perché? Ne parliamo nel Podcast di oggi di Tuttomercatoweb.com con Marco Conterio Nuovo passo in dirigenza: Calvelli nuovo CEO del Milan Prime dichiarazioni pubbliche da CEO del Milan per Massimo Calvelli, col dirigente che è stato annunciato tramite un comunicato del club rossonero. Queste le sue parole: "Guidare il Club in una fase decisiva del proprio percorso sportivo, in un momento altrettanto cruciale per l'intero calcio italiano, è una responsabilità che affronto con la massima serietà e con un profondo senso di urgenza. Il mandato ricevuto da Gerry è chiaro: riportare in AC Milan una cultura della vittoria e dei risultati, dentro e fuori dal campo, all'altezza della storia che questo Club rappresenta nel calcio europeo e dei suoi oltre 600 milioni di tifosi nel mondo.

title: La storia del Canada e una prima volta assoluta. Con Davies e David i giorni più belli
excerpt: Partirà ufficialmente questa sera con la sfida delle 21.
summary: Partirà ufficialmente questa sera con la sfida delle 21.00 tra Sudafrica e Canada il programma dei sedicesimi di finale della Coppa del Mondo 2026. Per entrambe sarà una prima volta assoluta, la prima partita nella fase a eliminazione diretta per due nazionali passate da seconde nei rispettivi raggruppamenti. Il Sudafrica del CT belga Hugo Broos stacca questo traguardo alla sua quarta partecipazione, il Canada dello statunitense Jesse Marsch invece dopo aver incassato solo sconfitte sia nel 1986 che nel 2022. Quella della nazionale canadese è la storia di una squadra che sta vivendo adesso il suo picco più alto. La storia di una squadra figlia di uno sport minore che solo con la generazione degli Alphonso Davies e dei Jonathan David sta riuscendo a trovare le luci della ribalta, a farsi spazio anche grazie alla nascita della Canadian Premier League tra sport più blasonati. Ospitare il Mondiale per la prima volta sta senza dubbio aiutando il movimento a crescere, soprattutto a ottenere una visibilità mai avuta prima. Stadi pieni e grande entusiasmo per i ragazzi di Jesse Marsch che però, a partire da stasera, cominceranno a giocare negli Stati Uniti. Tornando a ciò che è successo in passato, il Canada ha iniziato a gareggiare per le qualificazioni al Mondiale dal 1958 e ha staccato il pass per la prima volta nel 1986 dopo aver vinto un anno prima la Gold Cup contro l'Honduras. La seconda vittoria nella competizione arriverà invece nel 2000, in finale contro una Colombia invita...

title: Mondiali, il recap del Gruppo F: tre qualificate, col Giappone più in alto della Svezia
excerpt: Il Gruppo F dei Mondiali 2026 ha premiato Olanda e Giappone, ma sorride anche la Svezia, che prosegue il proprio cammino grazie al ripescaggio tra le migliori terze.
summary: Il Gruppo F dei Mondiali 2026 ha premiato Olanda e Giappone, ma sorride anche la Svezia, che prosegue il proprio cammino grazie al ripescaggio tra le migliori terze. Gli olandesi hanno conquistato il primo posto con sette punti, frutto del pareggio all'esordio contro il Giappone e delle successive vittorie contro Svezia e Tunisia. Un percorso solido, impreziosito da dieci reti segnate, che ha consentito agli Oranje di chiudere al comando del raggruppamento. Alle loro spalle si è classificato il Giappone, rimasto imbattuto per tutta la fase a gironi. I nipponici hanno pareggiato contro Olanda e Svezia, trovando poi il successo decisivo contro la Tunisia che è valso il secondo posto e la qualificazione diretta ai sedicesimi di finale. Continua il torneo anche per la Svezia. Gli scandinavi hanno raccolto quattro punti grazie alla vittoria sulla Tunisia e al pareggio con il Giappone. La sconfitta contro l'Olanda non ha compromesso il cammino della squadra, ripescata tra le migliori terze.

title: Milan target Silva, Hjulmand and Casado after striking Goncalo Ramos deal

summary: Reports in Italy claim that Antonio Silva, Morten Hjulmand and Marc Casado are the next players on Milan’s wishlist following their agreement with PSG to sign Goncalo Ramos in a club-record deal. Milan raised eyebrows with their deal for PSG’s Ramos, with some reports in Italy claiming that the Rossoneri will pay €74m in fixed fees for the Portugal international, who didn’t start a single game in the Champions League for his current club this season. It is a deal that has left some feeling not just surprised, but suspicious as well.

summary: Luka Modric, Martin Baturina, Nikola Vlasic, Petar Sucic and Marin Pongracic represent Serie A in the Croatia starting XI against Ghana, while England reshuffle to take on Panama. They both kick off at 22.00 UK time (23.00 CEST) and these fixtures will complete World Cup Group L. England had been expected to sweep the group comfortably after beating Croatia 4-2 in the opener, but their 0-0 draw with Ghana has made it a more balanced affair.

title: Fiorentina agreement for Beltran, but striker resists River Plate
excerpt: Fiorentina have agreed to sell half of Lucas Beltran to River Plate following the loan...
summary: Fiorentina have agreed to sell half of Lucas Beltran to River Plate following the loan at Valencia, but it’s reported the player wants to remain in Europe. The striker spent time in the River Plate academy and made his mark with the club, earning a €19.7m transfer to Fiorentina in the summer of 2023. Last season, he was told to find himself a new club, but was determined to continue his career in Europe rather than return to South America.

title: Inter not ready to improve Solet offer, as Atletico Madrid could swoop
excerpt: Alarm bells are ringing again for Inter, who don’t seem ready to go above their...
summary: Alarm bells are ringing again for Inter, who don’t seem ready to go above their rejected €20m offer for Udinese defender Oumar Solet, as Atletico Madrid prepare to get involved. It has been a series of disappointments on the transfer market for the Nerazzurri, who have placed very strict limits on their spending, despite winning the Serie A and Coppa Italia Double. Marco Palestra was snatched away when Chelsea came in with double their wage offer and €10m more to Atalanta, while they now risk losing another target too.

title: Atalanta defender Hien out of 2026 World Cup with serious injury
excerpt: Atalanta defender Isak Hien has seen his World Cup adventure cut short by a serious...
summary: Atalanta defender Isak Hien has seen his World Cup adventure cut short by a serious injury, as he is forced to leave the Sweden camp. ‘Unfortunately this dream ends prematurely.’ The centre-back had been in the starting XI for all three group matches, playing in the 5-1 victory over Tunisia, 5-1 defeat to the Netherlands, and 1-1 draw with Japan. However, the latter only lasted for 37 minutes, and he was forced to limp off due to an injury.

title: Where does Scotland's World Cup campaign leave Clarke?
excerpt: Did Steve Clarke get the best out of his Scotland squad at the 2026 World Cup as they face group-stage exit?
summary: Scotland head coach Steve Clarke signed a new four-year contract before the World Cup Steve Clarke ended Scotland's long World Cup drought but aspirations of a first taste of the knockout rounds - heightened by the expanded format - now rest on a series of highly unlikely results elsewhere. The draw was brutally tough, with Brazil and Morocco at five and six in Fifa's rankings. No other group had two top 10 teams.

title: Gakpo asks for privacy after loss of unborn son
excerpt: Noa van der Bij, the partner of Liverpool and Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo, announces the pair have lost their unborn son.
summary: Cody Gakpo, pictured with partner Noa van der Bij in 2022, is playing in his second World Cup for the Netherlands Liverpool and Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo has asked for "privacy and space" following the loss of his unborn son. His partner, model Noa van der Bij, announced the news on Saturday. The couple were expecting a second child together, with the baby due in October.

title: Japan and Morocco face old order giants in the hope of a brave new world | Jonathan Wilson
excerpt:
Two eye-catching last-32 ties offer hope of a winner from outside the traditional powers – Brazil and the Netherlands could be gone

The World Cup exists in a state of perpetual flux. It goes to new territories. It gets bigger. It experiments with second group phases and replaces playoffs fo
summary: Two eye-catching last-32 ties offer hope of a winner from outside the traditional powers – Brazil and the Netherlands could be gone The World Cup exists in a state of perpetual flux. It goes to new territories. It gets bigger. It experiments with second group phases and replaces playoffs for sides level on points with goal difference then head-to-head. And still one of the same eight countries from western Europe or South America wins it. Since Argentina in 1978, there have been two new winners, and those were France and Spain, from the heart of Uefa, their success based on maximising the advantages of being European and wealthy; no countries have been so successful at industrialising youth production, so much so that they now provide the models for every country seeking to invest in academy programmes.

title: Spain reveal Pino avoided fracture and retain World Cup hope for him and Williams
excerpt:
• Yéremy Pino and Nico Williams injuries are ‘moderate’

  • They were hurt during physical victory over Uruguay

Spain issued a more positive update than expected on Yéremy Pino and Nico Williams on Saturday, describing their injuries as moderate. Pino had been exp
summary: Yéremy Pino and Nico Williams injuries are ‘moderate’ They were hurt during physical victory over Uruguay Spain issued a more positive update than expected on Yéremy Pino and Nico Williams on Saturday, describing their injuries as moderate. Pino had been expected to miss the rest of the World Cup and there were concerns they had lost Nico Williams too, as qualification for the last 32 came at a heavy price.

title: Series slipping away from England as New Zealand strengthen position
excerpt: England lost their grip on the third Test against New Zealand, and likely the series, as the Black Caps got the better of a thrilling third day's play that saw 11 wickets tumble at Trent Bridge.
summary: England lose three in first six overs to slip from 223-2 overnight to 234-5 before being bowled out for 354; Rachin Ravindra (60no) grows New Zealand's lead to 204 by stumps, three down; watch day four live on Sky Sports Cricket from 10.15am on Sunday (first ball 11am) Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player England lost their grip on the third Test against New Zealand, and likely the series, as the Black Caps got the better of a thrilling third day's play that saw 11 wickets tumble at Trent Bridge.
